description_long: This object consists of three wooden storage boxes, each with a weathered, moss-stained appearance, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The central box is open at the top, revealing a hollow interior, while the two flanking boxes are closed with solid wooden lids. The wood planks are arranged vertically and horizontally, with visible grain, knots, and some greenish discoloration from moss or mildew. The boxes have a rustic, utilitarian design, with no visible handles, locks, or decorative elements. Their robust construction and aged look make them suitable for use in urban alleys, garages, or outdoor storage areas.
description: A set of three weathered wooden storage boxes, one open and two closed, with mossy stains and a rustic, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three boxes aligned, with the central box open and the two side boxes closed.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the open top of the central box and the solid lids of the two side boxes, with visible wood grain and moss stains.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, highlighting the closed backs of the boxes and the open central box.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the open central box and the closed side boxes, with more visible moss on the leftmost box.
attribute_lid_type: removable flat lid (for two boxes), open top (for central box)
